item.title: Space entrepreneurs borrow some surprisingly terrestrial tactics
item.description: Vertical integration helps to bridge the gap between today’s investments and returns that won’t come until later

item.title: Philip Morris allowed to market Zyn pouches in US as safer than smoking
item.description: Nicotine pouches are a focus of the tobacco industry’s push to grow beyond cigarettes

item.title: Key Burnham adviser pushes back at union call for bank tax rise
item.description: Trades Union Congress estimates restoring previous surcharge could raise £9bn

item.title: Schroders close to selling its financial planning arm for more than £200mn
item.description: UK asset manager aims to dispose of Benchmark Capital to Söderberg & Partners

item.title: Egg producers settle US claims they manipulated benchmark prices
item.description: Federal and state authorities cite three-year effort to co-ordinate bids that inflated quotations

item.title: Vedanta pulls listing of copper unit day before IPO
item.description: CopperTech Metals’ market entry was meant to take advantage of US enthusiasm for critical minerals

item.title: Binance and founder sued by crypto investors in London lawsuit
item.description: Nearly 1,700 UK users allege that world’s largest crypto exchange offered risky products without regulatory approval

item.title: Anthropic launches Claude Science in push for pharma revenue
item.description: New AI product has use cases in rendering 3D protein structures and drug discovery

item.title: AA shifts gears towards £5bn London listing
item.description: An IPO of UK roadside recovery business could pit it against rival flotation plans by RAC

item.title: Shetland Islands backs £1.5bn tunnels to boost population
item.description: Number of residents on outer islands of the Scottish archipelago has fallen by a quarter in forty years

item.title: Britain should put up more of a fight on the high cost of defence kit
item.description: A new mindset for procurement is needed as otherwise the big spending plan numbers amount to less than they promise

item.title: Starmer’s £15bn defence plan leaves short-term gaps, say former officers
item.description: UK’s large military investments are aimed at countering China, but questions remain about ability to withstand Russian threat

item.title: UK weighs intervention in Paramount’s $111bn Warner Bros takeover
item.description: Any regulatory referral on plurality grounds could delay or halt proceedings

item.title: KNDS struggles to convince investors to back IPO at €12bn-plus valuation
item.description: Blockbuster listing at risk of delay if desired price tag cannot be reached

item.title: Britain has an Oxbridge innovation problem
item.description: Universities challenged

item.title: BP deputy chief to leave company in latest upheaval at oil major
item.description: Carol Howle, the trading chief appointed deputy CEO less than three months ago, is set to retire

item.title: Rio Tinto agrees new financial terms for $18bn copper mine project
item.description: Oyu Tolgoi is Mongolia’s biggest mining scheme and source of foreign investment

item.title: Ukraine argues it can legally attack Russia’s shadow fleet
item.description: Kyiv seeks to justify strikes on tankers after Russia accuses it of ‘terrorism’ in letter to IMO

item.title: Tech CEOs want AI rules — it may be too late
item.description: A deregulated environment sounds good until it results in ad hoc political intervention

item.title: Battery metals futures heat up as volatility stirs markets
item.description: Trading of cobalt and lithium futures contracts has risen this year on the leading European and US exchanges

item.title: Starmer says some road and energy projects will be scrapped to fund £15bn defence plan
item.description: Publication of long-delayed blueprint is unlikely to end military figures’ calls for more funding

item.title: Apple and Google app store fees targeted by UK competition watchdog
item.description: CMA regulator launches consultation on whether Big Tech groups should allow rival payments

item.title: Comcast’s ‘amicable divorce’ is latest episode in Hollywood drama
item.description: NBCUniversal split sparks talk of future deals amid ongoing media and entertainment consolidation

item.title: EY employee charged with accessing Australian prime minister’s bank details
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers describes situation as ‘incredibly concerning’

item.title: Barclays buys Canary Wharf HQ for £750mn
item.description: UK bank buys 999-year lease on London headquarters in one of Europe’s biggest office deals in recent years

item.title: Sainsbury’s sales growth slows as consumer caution hits retailers
item.description: Rival supermarket Tesco also reports slowdown

item.title: Scottish Power calls on regulator to securitise bad UK energy debts
item.description: Household energy debts hit £4.8bn in the UK, adding more than £50 a year to the average bill

item.title: FT Alphaville’s AI Prediction World Cup: Post-group update: Predictions are predictably hard
item.description: Many stats; no learnings

item.title: EU must choose which sectors to protect or face exodus, warns Covestro chief
item.description: Comments come as Abu Dhabi-owned German group announces investments of up to €4bn in China and the UAE

item.title: FirstFT: How Elliott spawned a new army of activists
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: corporate AI users’ staff hires and Maersk raises profit guidance
